text: Iran Observer summarized that the 5th round of US-Iran nuclear talks in Rome on May 23 ended without a deal, with Oman FM al-Busaidi describing "some but not conclusive progress." Iran's Araghchi publicly stated before the talks that zero enrichment means "we do NOT have a deal" — making the enrichment issue the core unsolved obstacle entering Trump's NSC deliberations.
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour and RFERL confirmed 5th round concluded. Araghchi quote confirmed by Outlook India. Busaidi quote confirmed by PBS.

text: Axios National Security reported that Trump convened a senior NSC war council on May 23 morning, with Vance, Hegseth, CIA Director Ratcliffe, and Susie Wiles present to weigh whether to resume strikes against Iran. A source told Axios that Trump has raised the possibility of a "decisive" major military operation after which he could declare victory — the clearest signal yet of the administration's strike vs. deal calculus.
verificationNote: Axios confirmed with sources familiar with the meeting. Times of Israel liveblog May 23 corroborated.

text: Lebanon Conflict Tracker reported that Hezbollah fired rockets at an IDF military base in northern Israel on May 23, wounding 8 soldiers including Finance Minister Smotrich's son. In a separate simultaneous attack, a Hezbollah anti-tank missile wounded 2 more IDF soldiers in south Lebanon. Israeli strikes on Tyre earlier May 23 wounded 37 people including 9 women and 4 children, per Lebanon Health Ministry. Lebanon total: 3,095 killed, 9,364 wounded.
verificationNote: Times of Israel May 23 liveblog confirmed. Al Jazeera May 23 liveblog confirmed Tyre wounded. Lebanese Health Ministry cited for figures.

text: Intel Crawler flagged that Iran's military reconstitution during the April 8 ceasefire has significantly exceeded US intelligence community timelines — with drone production already restarted and 90% of underground missile facilities operational, per CNN's May 21 report citing two US officials. This assessment is the primary intelligence driver behind Trump's renewed consideration of strikes, creating urgency to either close a deal or act before Iran's capabilities fully rebuild.
verificationNote: CNN confirmed with two US officials. Antiwar.com corroborated. IRGC publicly acknowledged reconstitution in May 21 Press TV statement.

text: MidEast Watch noted that Mojtaba Khamenei — son of Iran's Supreme Leader and increasingly the hardline power behind the throne — issued a directive banning the export of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile abroad. This directly blocks a key US negotiating demand (verified by Reuters May 21-22) and forecloses the option of a deal that leaves Iran with a zero-enrichment status. The directive has been interpreted as an effort to prevent any negotiated outcome that Tehran's hardliners view as surrender.
verificationNote: Reuters reported May 21-22. Confirmed by The Researchers, Voice of Emirates, multiple aggregators.

text: Hormuz Shipping Tracker noted a UKMTO advisory issued May 23 of an armed small craft with 5 people approaching a product tanker ~200nm west of Socotra Island — the vessel's armed security team was deployed and the craft broke off. Hormuz remains effectively closed at Day 82. Iran's toll system (M/vessel in yuan) is operational. UK has deployed drone assets and a Royal Navy warship to join the US-led Project Freedom escort mission.
verificationNote: UKMTO advisory confirmed by Iran International liveblog. UK deployment confirmed by Al Jazeera May 23.

text: Conflict Watch reported that Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf delivered the sharpest military threat from Tehran since the April 8 ceasefire, warning Pakistan's Army Chief Munir that Iran's armed forces have 'rebuilt themselves during the ceasefire' and that any US resumption of war will be met with 'more crushing and bitter' consequences — effectively confirming the CNN/intelligence assessment that Iran's reconstitution has exceeded all US projections.
verificationNote: Ghalibaf warning confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog (May 23) and ANI News. Pakistan confirmed Munir's Tehran meetings.

text: Diplomacy Tracker flagged a major diplomatic surge on May 23: Qatar re-engaged as mediator — a Qatari negotiating team arrived in Tehran (Reuters exclusive) — alongside Pakistan's Munir, who met Araghchi, Ghalibaf, and Pezeshkian. Separately, Saudi Crown Prince MBS, UAE's MBZ, and Qatar's Tamim bin Hamad are all calling Trump directly to urge more time for diplomacy. Six weeks of parallel mediation may be approaching a decisive moment.
verificationNote: Qatar team confirmed by Reuters. Gulf leaders urging Trump confirmed by The National. Munir meetings confirmed by Tribune India and IranWire.

text: Northern Front Israel Tracker reported that Hezbollah killed a woman in northern Israel with rocket fire on May 23 — raising the confirmed Israeli civilian death toll to 25 — and separately severely wounded an IDF soldier with an explosive drone in southern Lebanon. The IDF labelled the drone attack a ceasefire violation. The incidents occurred hours after Hezbollah rockets wounded 8 IDF soldiers including Finance Minister Smotrich's son, in what is becoming the most violent phase of Lebanon front activity since the April 8 ceasefire.
verificationNote: Woman killed confirmed by Ynet News and Times of Israel. IDF soldier severely wounded confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog.

text: Strike Signal Watch noted that Trump skipping son Donald Jr.'s Bahamas wedding — combined with US military and intelligence officials canceling Memorial Day weekend plans and emergency contact lists being updated at US bases abroad — constitutes the clearest public signal yet of imminent US strike preparations. Analysts note that Trump has historically used personal schedule decisions as coercive signaling. No final decision has been confirmed; talks continue with Rome concluding without breakthrough and Qatar/Pakistan teams in Tehran.
verificationNote: Trump wedding cancellation confirmed by CBS News, WION. US military leave cancellations confirmed by Haaretz and Axios.

text: Beqaa Valley Intelligence reported the IDF conducted pre-dawn strikes on a Hezbollah underground weapons manufacturing and storage facility in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley on May 23. The Military Intelligence Directorate guided the operation under Northern Command. The Beqaa Valley has been targeted repeatedly since March as the IDF seeks to interdict Hezbollah's weapons production pipeline. Separately, IDF struck 30+ Hezbollah sites across southern Lebanon in the 24-hour window, including rocket launch sites, observation posts, and command infrastructure.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed Beqaa Valley strike via official channels, confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. 30+ strikes confirmed by Jerusalem Post.

text: IranWarLive confirmed on Day 85 that Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir completed a marathon 24-hour mediation session in Tehran, meeting separately with FM Araghchi, President Pezeshkian, and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf. Pakistan described talks as "highly productive" but Iran FM said differences remain "deep and significant." Munir departed Tehran Saturday carrying a fresh Iranian counter-proposal to the US.
verificationNote: Pakistan ISPR statement on Munir visit confirmed by Tribune India and ARY News. FM Baghaei statement on differences confirmed by Al Jazeera.

text: Nuclear Watch OSINT reported the core US-Iran impasse on Day 85 remains structurally unresolved: Mojtaba Khamenei issued a formal directive that Iran's ~440 kg of 60%-enriched uranium cannot leave the country under any deal, while Trump reiterated the US "will get the highly enriched uranium." These positions are mutually exclusive. A narrower war-ending MOU deferring nuclear issues to a 60-day follow-on period may be the only viable bridging mechanism.
verificationNote: Mojtaba Khamenei uranium directive confirmed by Reuters May 21. Trump HEU demand confirmed by CBS News and Axios May 23. 14-clause MOU framework confirmed by Iran FM Baghaei per Fortune.

text: Critical Threats assessed that Trump faces a binary decision by Sunday (May 24): accept a Memorandum of Understanding covering war cessation and Hormuz — deferring nuclear issues for 60 days — or resume Operation Epic Fury strikes. Trump convened an NSC war council Saturday including Vance, Hegseth, Ratcliffe, and Wiles. White House Deputy Chief Dan Scavino posted a B-2 stealth bomber video with no caption, widely interpreted as an implicit strike signal.
verificationNote: NSC war council confirmed by Axios. Scavino B-2 bomber post confirmed by Fox News live updates. Trump 50/50 quote from Axios exclusive May 23.

text: UN Security Council Watch noted two competing Hormuz resolutions are in circulation on Day 85: (1) a US-Bahrain draft demanding Iran halt attacks — facing likely Russia-China veto; (2) a French-drafted alternative proposing an international maritime mission to restore freedom of navigation. France confirmed it consulted Washington and Tehran before drafting. Neither has come to a vote. The dueling drafts reflect deepening P5 division on how to handle the Hormuz crisis.
verificationNote: France UNSC draft confirmed by US News/Reuters, Arab News, and Times of Israel liveblog. US-Bahrain text stall confirmed by multiple outlets.

text: Israel Security Watch noted that Israel has been entirely excluded from US-Iran peace negotiations, alarming Netanyahu's coalition. Reports cite an unnamed Israeli official saying envoy Witkoff is "placing immense pressure on Trump not to resume the war." Netanyahu convened an emergency coalition meeting Saturday describing the emerging MOU as "very bad" for Israel — it omits nuclear dismantlement, HEU export requirements, and proxy rollback. Israel cannot strike Iran unilaterally without US permission and overflight rights.
verificationNote: Netanyahu emergency coalition meeting confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. "Very bad" characterization confirmed by unnamed Israeli official per ToI. Witkoff pressure confirmed by same source. Israeli overflight dependency confirmed by Al Jazeera analysis.

text: Critical Threats/AEI reports Trump posted on Truth Social that the Iran deal is 'largely negotiated' and Hormuz will open 'shortly.' NSC war council convened Saturday with Vance, Hegseth, Ratcliffe, and Wiles. White House Deputy Chief Dan Scavino posted a B-2 stealth bomber video with no caption — analysts interpret it as an implicit military signal while Trump deliberates.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social post confirmed by NPR, CNBC, PBS May 23. Scavino B-2 post confirmed by Fox News live. NSC war council confirmed by Axios.

text: NukeWatch OSINT highlights the core US-Iran impasse: Mojtaba Khamenei issued a formal directive that Iran's ~440 kg of 60%-enriched uranium cannot leave the country under any deal, while Trump simultaneously demands the US 'will get the highly enriched uranium.' The two positions are mutually exclusive — an MOU deferring nuclear issues to 60-day follow-on talks may be the only viable bridge.
verificationNote: Mojtaba HEU directive confirmed by Reuters May 21 via Jerusalem Post live updates. Trump HEU demand confirmed by CBS News and Axios May 23.

text: Israel Security Watch reports that Israel has been entirely excluded from US-Iran peace deal negotiations. Netanyahu convened an emergency coalition meeting calling the emerging MOU 'very bad' — it omits nuclear dismantlement, HEU export requirements, and proxy rollback. Envoy Witkoff is reportedly placing 'immense pressure on Trump not to resume the war.' Simultaneously, Hezbollah FPV drones struck Iron Dome launchers at Biranit/Jal al-Alam — the 9th IDF soldier has been killed since the Lebanon ceasefire.
verificationNote: Netanyahu 'very bad' and exclusion confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Witkoff pressure confirmed by same source. Hezbollah FPV Iron Dome strike confirmed by Al Mayadeen and Jerusalem Post.

text: UNSC Watch reports two competing Hormuz resolutions remain in circulation with no vote scheduled: a US-Bahrain draft demanding Iran halt attacks (faces likely Russia-China veto) and a French-drafted alternative proposing an international maritime mission. P5 division deepening. EU on May 22 formally expanded sanctions framework to target Iran's Hormuz blockade actions; Kallas confirmed widening sanctions 'to include Hormuz.'
verificationNote: UNSC resolution standoff confirmed by Arab News, US News/Reuters. EU Hormuz sanctions expansion confirmed by Brussels Signal and EEAS press conference May 22.

text: War Monitor confirms Hezbollah released footage of FPV kamikaze drones striking Iron Dome launcher batteries at Biranit barracks (Jal al-Alam site), northern Israel. Maintenance personnel filmed fleeing just before impact. Multiple batteries hit. Separately: Israel struck 6 southern Lebanon towns in 24 hours killing 16+ civilians including children — Al-Baqbouq (5), Seir al-Gharbiya (5), Deir Qanoun (4), Al-Housh (1), Al-Rafid (1). Lebanese Army Nabatieh barracks also struck.
verificationNote: Hezbollah FPV Iron Dome strike confirmed by Al Mayadeen video, Jerusalem Post, and The Defense News. Lebanon strikes confirmed by Al Jazeera and Lebanon Health Ministry May 23.

text: Hormuz Fleet Watch: IRGC Navy announced 25 commercial vessels transited Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours under IRGC coordination and 'security supervision' — continuing the IRGC's de facto tollbooth system. UAE East-West bypass pipeline is now 50% complete (as of May 20). Trump's 'largely negotiated' deal post signals Hormuz reopening may be days away if deal closes, but Iran still insists on controlled-access conditions rather than free passage.
verificationNote: 25-ship IRGC transit figure from Press TV May 23. UAE pipeline 50% completion from CNBC May 20. IRGC coordinated access confirmed by Al Jazeera and Safety4Sea.
